item.title: My Aunt Pretends My Cousin Doesn’t Have a Drug Problem. Do I Have to Play Along?
item.description: I am sick of the charade we are expected to maintain around them.

item.title: How a Gang of Thieves Pulled Off a Multimillion-Dollar Data Center Heist
item.description: The world’s most valuable assets are stored on rows of servers in giant, anonymous buildings. And they can be stolen.

item.title: There’s an Injury Epidemic in Pro Sports. There’s Also a Recovery Revolution.
item.description: Athletes are pushing their bodies to astonishing feats, knowing that medical advances can heal them quickly.

item.title: I’m Worried My Kids Will Regret Cutting Off Their Dad. Should I Say Something?
item.description: They know he has health problems, but I’m not sure they fully grasp that death can come suddenly.

item.title: The Long-Running, Never-Ending Argument Over What Counts as ‘America’
item.description: The label applies to the country, but also two whole continents. Our claim over it is yet another way for us to annoy the entire hemisphere.

item.title: Is My Wife’s Sister’s Husband Really My Brother-In-Law?
item.description: A ruling on the limits of familial bonds.

item.title: My Abuser’s Daughter Wants to Be Friends. How Should I Navigate This?
item.description: I feel deeply awkward, especially because I genuinely like her.

item.title: Why Epidemics Breed Rage at Health Workers
item.description: Some people have fought Ebola containment measures in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Scholars have seen similar responses across societies and throughout history.

item.title: My Stepdaughter’s College Was Paid For. Do We Have to Cover Grad School?
item.description: She’s angry that we saved for her siblings’ college educations but not for hers.

item.title: My (Crazy, Heartbreaking, Glorious) World Cup Adventure
item.description: Soccer is perfect for screens. But being there in person showed me a side of the sport — and of my fellow human beings — that I’ll never forget.

item.title: Zohran Mamdani Knows He Has Political Capital. And He Intends to Spend It.
item.description: Lulu Garcia-Navarro sits down for an interview with the mayor of New York City.

item.title: My Relationship With My Brother Was Fraught. Could Sibling Therapy Help Us?
item.description: I was drawn to and worried about this sort of therapy — maybe there were dangers in stirring things up.

item.title: Why Does This Jacket Cost $770? Because It Made Me a New Man.
item.description: Lots of men are geeking out over very expensive clothes — and I became one of them.

item.title: Bill Maher Feuded With Trump. Did He Get the Last Laugh?
item.description: How the late-night host won comedy’s biggest prize — despite the White House’s objections.
